theory.canonical_statement: xt{input}} E_k^\dagger \end{equation} Channel capacity follows from Holevo bound: \begin{equation} C_{\text{quantum}} = \max_{\rho_{\text{input}}} [S(\rho_{\text{output}}) - S(\rho_{\text{output}|\text{input}})] \end{equation} \subsection{Semantic Compression via Persistent Homology} Trajectory information compresses through topological data analysis. Persistence diagrams encode topological features persisting across scale changes. \subsection{Quantum Error Correction with CSS Codes} Transmitted quantum trajectories undergo error correction through Calderbank-Shor-Steane codes with parameters $[n,k,d] = [153600, 76800, \sqrt{153600}]$. \subsection{Bandwidth Optimization} Classical information capacity bounds follow Shannon-Hartley: \begin{equation} C_{\text{classical}} = B \log_2(1 + \text{SNR}) \end{equation} Total communication latency includes encoding, transmission, and decoding. Fidelity quantifies reconstruction quality: \begin{equation} F = |\langle \psi_{\text{sent}} | \psi_{\text{received}} \rangle|^2 \end{equation} \title{Magneto-Acoustic Topology and Dimensional Resonance:\\ A Unified Field-Theoretic Framework} \a
theory.technical_interpretation: Definition of [N,k,d] is an algebraic definition, written [n,k,d] = [153600, 76800, √(153600)]. It is an algebraic definition expressing the quantity directly in terms of the variables on its right-hand side. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
